revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:近津神社 (埼玉県杉戸町)]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Chikatsu Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Chikatsu Jinja (Sugito) 01.JPG |location = {{ill|Sugito, Saitama|en|Sugito, Saitama|qid=Q1345351|lt=Sugito, Saitama|1=Sugito, Saitama}}, Kitakatsushika District, Saitama Prefecture, 1-1-29 Seichi |Latitude Degrees = 36 |Latitude Minutes = 01 |Latitude Seconds = 40.9 |Longitude Degrees = 139 |Longitude Minutes = 43 |Longitude Seconds = 59.9 |Map = |deity = {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}} |type = {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} |consecration_year = Unknown |Main Hall = |Alternate name = |Pilgrimage Sites = |festival = |Rituals = |location = {{ill|Sugito|qid=Q1345351|1=Sugito, Saitama}} |coordinates = {{Coord|36.028028|N|139.733306|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Chikatsu Shrine''' (近津神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Sugito, Saitama|en|Sugito, Saitama|qid=Q1345351|lt=Sugito, Saitama|1=Sugito, Saitama}}, Kitakatsushika District, Saitama Prefecture. == History == The founding date of Chikatsu Shrine is unknown. There are two main theories about its origin: one suggests that it was established by transferring part of the spirit from one of the {{ill|Chikatsu Shrines|ja|近津神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikatsu Shrines|qq=}} commonly found in {{ill|Fukushima Prefecture|en|Fukushima Prefecture|qid=Q71707|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|en|Ibaraki Prefecture|qid=Q83273|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}, and {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|qid=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}}. The other theory posits that it received its deity, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武甕槌命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}}, from {{ill|Kashima Shrine|en|Kashima Shrine|qid=Q706499|lt=Kashima Shrine|1=Kashima Jingū}} in {{ill|Hitachi Province|en|Hitachi Province|qid=Q1055353|lt=Hitachi Province|1=Hitachi Province}} (present-day Ibaraki Prefecture).<ref name="Shrine">埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 大里・北葛飾・比企』埼玉県神社庁、1992nd year 、860-861p</ref> Two temples historically associated with the shrine are "Raigōin" and "Shihōin." Raigōin was a temple of the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|en|Shingon Buddhism|qid=Q550182|lt=Shingon school|1=Shingon Buddhism}}, affiliated with {{ill|Hōshō-ji Temple (Satte)|ja|宝聖寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōshō-ji Temple (Satte)|1=Hōshō-ji Temple (disambiguation)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q124481885}} in {{ill|Satte, Saitama|en|Satte, Saitama|qid=Q868971|lt=Satte, Saitama|1=Satte, Saitama}}. Shihōin, meanwhile, belonged to the {{ill|Tōzan-ha|ja|当山派|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōzan-ha|1=Tōzan-ha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11487826}} school of {{ill|Shugendō|en|Shugendō|qid=Q1483875|lt=Shugendō|1=Shugendō}}. Raigōin served as the shrine's {{ill|bettō|ja|別当寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bettō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Bettō-ji|qid=Q11396755}}, while Shihōin conducted {{ill|sutra chanting|ja|読経|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sutra chanting|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Buddhist chant|qid=Q28163991}} and {{ill|kaji kito|ja|加持祈祷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kaji kito|1=kaji kito|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11398638}} at the shrine. Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|qid=Q1970535|lt=Shinto-Buddhism separation|1=Shinbutsu bunri}} policy of the early {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era}}, both temples were abolished, and the monk from Shihōin secularized to become the shrine's priest.<ref name="Shrine"></ref> In {{ill|1873|en|1873|qid=Q7757|lt=1873|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), the shrine was designated a "village shrine" under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. In {{ill|1893|en|1893|qid=Q7840|lt=1893|1=1893}} (Meiji 26), {{ill|1908|en|1908|qid=Q2056|lt=1908|1=1908}} (Meiji 41), and {{ill|1912|en|1912|qid=Q2077|lt=1912|1=1912}} (Taisho 1), a total of five shrines were merged into Chikatsu Shrine.<ref name="Shrine"></ref> == Access == * 13-minute walk from {{ill|Tōbu-Dōbutsu-Kōen Station|en|Tōbu-Dōbutsu-Kōen Station|qid=Q843158|lt=Tōbu Dōbutsu Kōen Station|1=Tōbu-Dōbutsu-Kōen Station}}. == References == {{Reflist|qq=}} == Bibliography == * 埼玉県神社庁神社調査団 編『埼玉の神社 大里・北葛飾・比企』埼玉県神社庁、1992nd year {{Pref-stub|pref=Saitama Prefecture|qq=}} {{Shinto-stub|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|近津神社 (埼玉県杉戸町)|version=103198942|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:近津神社 (埼玉県杉戸町)]] [[ja:近津神社]]
